mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and housing starts signal a broad housing sector contraction that drags down consumer spending and equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind, signaling peak earnings that precede negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during late-cycle expansions correlates with positive 60-day SPX returns, or the mechanism fails to outperform a baseline model in regime-specific walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive relationship with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation exceeds 0.3%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal exhibits Rank IC < 0.03 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, decelerating housing starts signal persistent sector weakness that drags on related equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST deceleration during recovery regimes correlates with positive 20-day SPX returns, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.
